body.title: CLOSED: Igor playbook memory type
body.result: playbook.py CRUD + NE injection + dreaming playbook proposals; 12 tests green
body.description:
Add PLAYBOOK as a new memory_type value in clan.memories. A playbook is a structured text artifact: conditions (when to apply) + heuristics (what to do). Dreaming proposes playbooks via instance.proposals queue; a new `playbook.py` module (direct psycopg2, same pattern as watch_problems.py) provides add_playbook() / read_active_playbooks() / archive_playbook(). NE loads active playbooks at prompt construction time as a context block (injected by narrative_engine.py, budget-aware).

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/playbook.py` (new), `wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py` (playbook context block injection), `wild_igor/igor/cognition/dreaming.py` (write playbooks)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2 (no cortex.py touch — avoids HIGH-inertia); never hard-delete (use active=false); NE prompt must stay within token budget (cap playbook block at 500 tokens); source field records which dreaming run created each playbook
**Scope boundary:** IN — playbook.py CRUD, NE injection, dreaming proposal path (via instance.proposals — not direct clan.memories writes). OUT — no cortex.py integration (future ticket), no playbook UI, no manual playbook creation tool yet.
**Test plan:** add_playbook() inserts row; read_active_playbooks() returns only active rows; archive_playbook() sets active=false; NE prompt construction includes playbook block when active playbooks exist
